segments.name: Agents

segments.name: AI Browsers
segments.description: Browsers built or adapted specifically for AI agent use, including headless variants optimised for programmatic navigation.

segments.description: General-purpose automation tools and frameworks, including GUI-driven and script-driven flows.

segments.description: Tools that let agents query backend systems, databases and APIs.

segments.name: SaaS Builders

children.name: Code-Defined
children.description: Hosted builders where agents and workflows are primarily defined in code (SDK, DSL, or config-as-code).

children.name: No Code
children.description: Builders targeted at non-programmers, typically form- and config-driven with minimal visual wiring.

children.name: Visual Programming
Workflow Focused
children.description: Visual builders oriented around structured, multi-step workflows rather than free-form agents.
Builders where flows are expressed as node-and-edge graphs or canvas-based visual programs.

segments.description: Hosted SaaS platforms for building agents and agentic workflows, spanning no-code, visual, and code-defined paradigms.

children.name: Emerging (Ecosystem-Tied)

children.description: Standards with a published specification and active governance.

segments.description: Agents and tools built for a single specific task rather than general use.
